6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
10	
11	/-!
12	# Čech bridge — assembly layer
13	
14	This file is the downstream assembly layer that consumes both:
15	
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex` (from `PresheafCech.lean`): the section Čech
17	  cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F) : CochainComplex Ab ℕ` built cosimplicially from
18	  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.
19	
20	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex` (from `FreePresheafComplex.lean`): the
21	  free-presheaf chain complex `K(𝒰)_• : ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose
22	  degree-`p` term is `∐_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
23	
24	The reason this file must sit downstream of both is that `FreePresheafComplex.lean`
25	already imports `PresheafCech.lean`, so any file needing both must import
26	`FreePresheafComplex.lean` (which transitively brings in `PresheafCech.lean`).
27	
28	## Declarations (to be filled by the prover)
29	
30	- `cechComplex_hom_identification` (planned): a per-degree `Ab`-isomorphism
31	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)` intertwining the differentials, assembled with
32	  `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`.
33	
34	- `injective_cech_acyclic` (planned): Čech cohomology vanishes in positive degrees for
35	  injective sheaves, established via `cechComplex_hom_identification`.
36	
37	- `ses_cech_h1` (planned): the short exact sequence in Čech H¹ induced by a short exact
38	  sequence of sheaves.
39	
40	- `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` / `affine_serre_vanishing` (planned): the comparison
41	  isomorphism `Ȟ•(𝒰, F) ≅ H•(X, F)` on affine schemes / for acyclic covers, leading to
42	  Serre's vanishing theorem.
43	-/
44	
45	universe u
46	
47	open CategoryTheory Limits
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
50	
51	/-
52	Planner strategy for `cechComplex_hom_identification`:
53	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54	
55	Goal: construct a cochain-complex isomorphism
56	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`
57	where:
58	  • `K(𝒰)_p = ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
59	    (the degree-`p` term of `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`)
60	  • `Č^p(𝒰, F) = ∏_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
61	    (the degree-`p` term of `sectionCechComplex 𝒰 F`)
62	  Both complexes live in `Ab` after applying the global-sections functor.
63	
64	Step 1 — per-degree hom-coproduct duality:
65	  For each `p`, the Yoneda/free-presheaf adjunction gives
66	    `Hom(freeYoneda.obj V, F) ≅ F.obj V`  (natural in `V`).
67	  This is exactly `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` from `PresheafCech.lean`.
68	  Combined with the coproduct-hom identity
69	    `Hom(∐_σ A_σ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(A_σ, F)`
70	  (via `preadditiveYoneda` preservation of limits, or hand-rolled from
71	  `Limits.Sigma.desc` / `Limits.Sigma.ι`), we get a degree-wise `Ab`-iso
72	    `Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) ≅ ∏_σ F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
73	
74	Step 2 — differential intertwining:
75	  Show that the degree-wise isos commute with the differentials.  The differential on
76	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)` is precomposition with the Čech boundary `d_p : K(𝒰)_{p+1} → K(𝒰)_p`
77	  (the alternating sum of face maps of `cechFreeSimplicial`).  The differential on
78	  `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the alternating sum of restriction maps built by `sectionCechComplex`.
79	  These match under the per-degree iso by naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
80	
81	Step 3 — assemble:
82	  Use `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents` (or equivalent) to combine the per-degree
83	  isos into a full cochain-complex iso, supplying the naturality squares from Step 2.
84	
85	Key API:
86	  • `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` (PresheafCech.lean): the per-degree iso.
87	  • `Limits.Sigma.desc`, `Limits.Sigma.ι`: coproduct universal property in
88	    `X.PresheafOfModules`.
89	  • `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`: assemble component-wise isos into a
90	    full complex iso.
91	  • `sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean): the target complex.
92	  • `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreePresheafComplex_X` (FreePresheafComplex.lean):
93	    the source complex and its degreewise unfolding lemma.
94	-/
95	
96	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech hom-identification -/
97	
98	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
99	
100	/-- **The hom cosimplicial abelian group `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)`.**
101	
102	The cosimplicial object in `Ab` obtained by applying the contravariant additive functor
103	`Hom_{PMod}(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` to the free Čech simplicial object
104	`cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰`.  Its value in degree `p` is the hom-group
105	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`, and its alternating-coface-map cochain complex is the left-hand
106	side of the Čech hom-identification.  Defining it this way makes the differential of the
107	hom-complex literally the alternating sum of `Hom(faceᵢ, F)`, so the identification with
108	`sectionCechComplex` reduces to a cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
109	
110	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `cechFreeSimplicial` nor this contravariant transport. -/
111	noncomputable def homCechCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
112	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} :=
113	  (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).rightOp ⋙ preadditiveYoneda.obj F
114	
115	/-- **The hom cochain complex `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)`** — the left-hand side of the Čech
116	hom-identification.
117	
118	The alternating-coface-map cochain complex of `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F`. Built the same way as
119	`sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean) so that the planned identification
120	`cechComplex_hom_identification : homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F` is just
121	`(alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso` of the cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
122	
123	Project-local: the hom-complex of the free Čech resolution has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
124	noncomputable def homCechComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
125	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
126	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F)
127	
128	/-- **Per-degree component of the Čech hom-identification.**
129	
130	For each simplicial degree `n`, the abelian-group isomorphism
131	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_n, F) ≅ ∏_σ F(⨅ₖ U(σ k))` assembled from (i) the coproduct–hom duality
132	`Hom(∐_σ Aσ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(Aσ, F)` (the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` preserves the limit,
133	so `piComparison` is an iso, combined with `opCoproductIsoProduct`), and (ii) the per-index
134	free–Yoneda section identification `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
135	
136	Project-local: this is the degreewise core of `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`. -/
137	noncomputable def homCechSectionIsoApp (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
138	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) :
139	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).obj n ≅ (sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F).obj n :=
140	  (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapIso
141	      (opCoproductIsoProduct
142	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))
143	    ≪≫ asIso (piComparison (preadditiveYoneda.obj F)
144	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => Opposite.op (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))))
145	    ≪≫ Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
146	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) F).toAddCommGrpIso)
147	
148	/-- `Pi.mapIso e` is by definition `Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom)`. -/
149	private lemma pi_mapIso_hom_eq {β : Type*} {C : Type*} [Category C] {f g : β → C}
150	    [HasProductsOfShape β C] (e : ∀ b, f b ≅ g b) :
151	    (Limits.Pi.mapIso e).hom = Limits.Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom) := rfl
152	
153	/-- Characterizing property of `homCechSectionIsoApp`: its `σ`-component is precomposition
154	with the coproduct injection `Sigma.ι σ` followed by the free–Yoneda section identification.
155	Reduces the naturality of the assembled iso to the naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`. -/
156	private lemma homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
157	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) (σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
158	    (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F n).hom ≫
159	        Pi.π (fun τ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
160	          F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))) σ =
161	      (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map
162	          (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
163	            freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) σ).op ≫
164	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom := by
165	  rw [homCechSectionIsoApp, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
166	    pi_mapIso_hom_eq]
167	  simp only [Category.assoc]
168	  erw [Pi.map_π, piComparison_comp_π_assoc]
169	  rw [← Category.assoc]
170	  congr 1
171	  exact ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
172	    (congrArg _ (opCoproductIsoProduct_hom_comp_π σ))
173	
174	/-- **Naturality of the free–Yoneda section identification in the open.**
175	
176	For an inclusion of opens `h : V ⟶ W`, precomposition with `freeYoneda.map h` on the hom-side
177	corresponds to restriction `F.presheaf.map h.op` on the section side.  This is the single
178	naturality square that powers the cosimplicial naturality of the Čech hom-identification. -/
179	private lemma freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality {V W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X}
180	    (h : V ⟶ W) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
181	    (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map (freeYoneda.map h).op ≫
182	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv V F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom
183	      = (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv W F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom ≫ F.presheaf.map h.op := by
184	  ext ψ
185	  show freeYonedaHomEquiv V F (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ)
186	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op)) (freeYonedaHomEquiv W F ψ)
187	  show yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ))
188	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op))
189	          (yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ))
190	  rw [show PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ)
191	        = yoneda.map h ≫ PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ from
192	      (PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction _).homEquiv_naturality_left (yoneda.map h) ψ,
193	    ← yonedaEquiv_naturality]
194	  rfl
195	
196	/-- **The cosimplicial natural isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.**
197	
198	Assembles the per-degree isomorphisms `homCechSectionIsoApp` into a natural isomorphism of
199	cosimplicial abelian groups. The naturality square reduces, via `homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π`
200	in both degrees, to the single naturality square `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality` of the
201	free–Yoneda section identification.
202	
203	Project-local: the cosimplicial comparison of the hom-complex with the section complex has no
204	Mathlib counterpart. -/
205	noncomputable def homCechSectionCosimplicialIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
206	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
207	    homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
208	  NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F) (by
209	    intro n m f
210	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
211	    intro σ
212	    dsimp only [sectionCechCosimplicial]
213	    rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π]
214	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F m σ]
215	    conv_rhs => rw [← Category.assoc]
216	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F n (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f))]
217	    erw [Category.assoc]
218	    erw [← freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality
219	        (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f) σ)) F]
220	    dsimp only [homCechCosimplicial, Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map]
221	    erw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
222	    congr 1
223	    congr 1
224	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
225	    simp only [unop_comp]
226	    dsimp only [cechFreeSimplicial]
227	    erw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
228	    rfl
229	    )
230	
231	/-- **The Čech hom-identification** (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`).
232	
233	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)` identifying the
234	hom-complex of the free Čech resolution with the section Čech complex of `F`. Obtained by
235	applying the alternating-coface-map complex functor to the cosimplicial natural isomorphism
236	`homCechSectionCosimplicialIso`, so the differential intertwining is automatic from the
237	cosimplicial naturality.
238	
239	Project-local: the comparison of the free-resolution hom-complex with the section complex has
240	no Mathlib counterpart. -/
241	noncomputable def cechComplex_hom_identification (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
242	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
243	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
244	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso (homCechSectionCosimplicialIso 𝒰 F)
245	
246	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
9	
10	/-!
11	# Free-presheaf Čech complex (P3b bridge — free-complex side)
12	
13	This file is the free-presheaf-complex side of the P3b bridge.  The section side lives in
14	`PresheafCech.lean`; this file owns the two free-complex declarations:
15	
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex`  (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`)
17	  — the chain complex of free presheaves of `O_X`-modules whose degree-`p` term is
18	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj`
19	  `  (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`.
20	
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`  (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`)
22	  — the free complex is a quasi-isomorphism / free resolution of `O_𝒰`.
23	-/
24	
25	universe u
26	
27	open CategoryTheory Limits
28	
29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
30	
31	/-
32	Planner strategy (P3b free-complex side; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
33	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
34	
35	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
37	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
39	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
40	with the alternating-face differential.
41	
42	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
43	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
44	   above direct sums.
45	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.
46	   This gives d²=0 automatically via `SimplicialObject.boundarySquareZero`.
47	
48	Key API:
49	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
50	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
51	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
52	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
53	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
54	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
55	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
56	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
57	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
58	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.
59	
60	DEAD END — do NOT hand-roll the alternating-sum identity for d²=0.  Use the simplicial
61	path above.
62	
63	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
64	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
65	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
66	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
67	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
68	
69	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
70	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
71	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
72	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
73	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices
74	   `σ` with `V ⊆ ⨅ k, U (σ k)`, and `0` otherwise.
75	3. Fix any `i_fix : ι` with `V ⊆ U i_fix` (if none exists, the complex is `0`).
76	   The homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` maps the `σ`-summand to the `(i_fix, σ)`-summand
77	   (prepend `i_fix`).  Check `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
78	4. Package as `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`, then use `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso`.
79	
80	Key API:
81	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`.
82	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — homotopy equivalence ⟹ quasi-iso.
83	
84	DEAD END — do NOT route through `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy`.  That
85	interface has a different index convention and is not directly applicable here.
86	-/
87	
88	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
89	
90	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
91	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
92	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
93	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
94	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
95	
96	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
97	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
98	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
99	
100	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
101	
102	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
103	
104	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
105	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
106	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
107	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
108	
109	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
110	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
111	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
112	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
113	
114	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
115	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
116	
117	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
118	
119	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
120	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
121	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
122	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
123	
124	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
125	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
126	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
127	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
128	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
129	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
130	
131	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
132	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
133	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
134	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
135	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
136	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
137	  subst e; simp
138	
139	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
140	
141	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
142	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
143	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
144	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
145	
146	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
147	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
148	
149	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
150	Mathlib counterpart. -/
151	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
152	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
153	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
154	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
155	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
156	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
157	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
158	  map_id n := by
159	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
160	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
161	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
162	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
163	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
164	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
165	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
166	      eqToHom_map]
167	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
168	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
169	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
170	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
171	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
172	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
173	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
174	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
175	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
176	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
177	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,

344	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms
345	
346	Homology in `PresheafOfModules R` is computed objectwise: the evaluation functors
347	`PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V` are jointly conservative and preserve homology, so a
348	morphism of complexes of presheaves of modules is a quasi-isomorphism as soon as each of its
349	evaluations is.  These three lemmas package that reduction.  Mathlib has the single-functor
350	statement `HomologicalComplex.quasiIso_map_iff_of_preservesHomology` (which needs the functor to
351	reflect isomorphisms — false for a single evaluation), but not the joint-conservativity version
352	needed to reduce a quasi-isomorphism of presheaf-of-module complexes to its sectionwise checks. -/
353	
354	open HomologicalComplex in
355	/-- For a functor `F` preserving homology, if the induced map on the homology of the mapped
356	complexes is an isomorphism, then `F` applied to the homology map is an isomorphism.  This is the
357	naturality square of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso` read off at a fixed degree. -/
358	private lemma isIso_Fmap_homologyMap {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {C₁ C₂ : Type*}
359	    [Category C₁] [Category C₂] [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive C₂]
360	    {K L : HomologicalComplex C₁ c} (φ : K ⟶ L) (F : C₁ ⥤ C₂) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]
361	    (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
362	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).HasHomology i]
363	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj L).HasHomology i]
364	    (hiso : IsIso (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i)) :
365	    IsIso (F.map (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap φ i)) := by
366	  have key := ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso_hom_naturality ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ) F
367	  haveI hmid : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
368	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := hiso
369	  change IsIso (F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)))
370	  haveI hcomp : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
371	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)) ≫
372	      (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj L).mapHomologyIso F).hom) := inferInstance
373	  haveI hcomp2 : IsIso ((((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom ≫
374	      F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := key ▸ hcomp
375	  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left
376	    (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom _
377	
378	/-- **Joint conservativity of evaluations.** A morphism of presheaves of `R`-modules is an
379	isomorphism as soon as each of its evaluations `(evaluation R V).map g` is, since the underlying
380	presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of presheaves is an
381	isomorphism iff it is so objectwise.  Project-local: the joint-conservativity packaging is not in
382	Mathlib. -/
383	private lemma isIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat)
384	    {M N : PresheafOfModules R} (g : M ⟶ N)
385	    (h : ∀ V, IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).map g)) : IsIso g := by
386	  suffices hh : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g) from
387	    isIso_of_reflects_iso g (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R)
388	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
389	  intro V
390	  have hV := h V
391	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map] at hV
392	  haveI : IsIso (g.app V) := hV
393	  have e : ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g).app V
394	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab).map (g.app V) := rfl
395	  rw [e]
396	  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab) (g.app V)
397	
398	/-- **Objectwise reduction of quasi-isomorphisms.** A morphism `φ` of chain complexes of
399	presheaves of `R`-modules is a quasi-isomorphism if, for every object `V`, its evaluation
400	`(evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex |>.map φ` (a morphism of complexes of `R(V)`-modules) is a
401	quasi-isomorphism.  This is the formal core of the sectionwise argument that the free Čech complex
402	resolves the cover structure presheaf: homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise.
403	
404	Project-local: combines `isIso_of_evaluation` (joint conservativity) with `isIso_Fmap_homologyMap`
405	(evaluation preserves homology). -/
406	lemma quasiIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat) {ι : Type*}
407	    {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules R) c} (φ : K ⟶ L)
408	    [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i]
409	    (h : ∀ V, QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ)) :
410	    QuasiIso φ := by
411	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
412	  intro i
413	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
414	  apply isIso_of_evaluation R
415	  intro V
416	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map]
417	  haveI hqV : QuasiIsoAt (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i :=
418	    (h V).quasiIsoAt i
419	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap] at hqV
420	  exact isIso_Fmap_homologyMap φ (PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V) i hqV
421	
422	end AlgebraicGeometry

980	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
981	    (Inverts.of_dvd (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j))
982	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))
983	
984	lemma cechCoface_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1))
985	    (x : cechCoeff s M r (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) :
986	    cechCoface s M r m σ j x = cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j x := rfl
987	
988	/-- The localised section Čech differential at index `r`, as a bundled `R`-linear
989	map (mirrors `dDiff` with the localised cofaces). -/
990	noncomputable def locDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
991	    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
992	  LinearMap.pi fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
993	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • (cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
994	
995	lemma locDiff_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ)
996	    (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
997	    locDiff s M r m t σ
998	      = ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
999	          (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) := by
1000	  rw [locDiff, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sum_apply]
1001	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun d _ => rfl
1002	
1003	/-- `locDiff` agrees, as a raw function, with the localised combinatorial
1004	differential `CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)` already shown exact. -/
1005	lemma locDiff_eq_depDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
1006	    ⇑(locDiff s M r m)
1007	      = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ)
1008	          (cechCoface s M r) (m := m) := by
1009	  funext t σ
1010	  rw [locDiff_apply]
1011	  simp only [CombinatorialCech.depDiff]
1012	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => by rw [cechCoface_apply]
1013	
1014	/-- Positive-degree exactness of the bundled localised differential, transported
1015	from `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`. -/
1016	lemma locDiff_exact (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
1017	    Function.Exact (locDiff s M r (m + 1)) (locDiff s M r (m + 2)) := by
1018	  rw [locDiff_eq_depDiff, locDiff_eq_depDiff]
1019	  exact CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact s M r m
1020	
1021	/-- The product localisation map `D^m → ∏_σ M_{s_r·s_σ}` at index `r`. -/
1022	noncomputable def fLoc (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
1023	    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
1024	  LinearMap.pi fun σ => dToCech s M r σ ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj σ
1025	
1026	lemma fLoc_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) (σ : Fin m → ι) :
1027	    fLoc s M r m t σ = dToCech s M r σ (t σ) := by
1028	  simp only [fLoc, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.proj_apply]
1029	
1030	/-- `fLoc` exhibits the product localised coefficient as the localisation of `D^m`
1031	away from `s_r` (from `IsLocalizedModule.pi` + `dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/
1032	instance fLoc_isLocalizedModule [Finite ι] (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
1033	    IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (fLoc s M r m) := by
1034	  haveI : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s r)) (dToCech s M r σ) :=
1035	    fun σ => dToCech_isLocalizedModule s M r σ
1036	  exact IsLocalizedModule.pi (Submonoid.powers (s r)) fun σ => dToCech s M r σ
1037	
1038	/-- The localised differential intertwines `fLoc` with `dDiff` (the `dToCech_comm`
1039	square, packaged through the bundled maps). -/
1040	lemma locDiff_fLoc (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :
1041	    locDiff s M r m (fLoc s M r m t) = fLoc s M r (m + 1) (dDiff s M m t) := by
1042	  have h1 : ⇑(locDiff s M r m) (fLoc s M r m t)
1043	      = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r) (m := m)
1044	          (fun σ => dToCech s M r σ (t σ)) := by
1045	    rw [locDiff_eq_depDiff]
1046	    exact congrArg _ (funext fun σ => fLoc_apply s M r m t σ)
1047	  rw [h1, dToCech_comm]
1048	  funext σ
1049	  exact (fLoc_apply s M r (m + 1) (dDiff s M m t) σ).symm
1050	
1051	/-- `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `dDiff` is the bundled localised differential
1052	`locDiff` — by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`, the comparison reducing to `locDiff_fLoc`.
1053	Stated for an arbitrary away element `a` (with `s r = a`) so the spanning-element
1054	bookkeeping in `dDiff_exact` needs no `↑ρ`-rewrite inside the localised map. -/
1055	lemma map_dDiff_eq_locDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) {a : R}
1056	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m)]
1057	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))] :
1058	    IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))
1059	        (dDiff s M m) = locDiff s M r m := by
1060	  apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m)
1061	    (fun x => IsLocalizedModule.map_units (fLoc s M r (m + 1)) x)
1062	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro t
1063	  rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, IsLocalizedModule.map_apply, locDiff_fLoc]
1064	
1065	/-- A chosen `ι`-index realising a spanning-set element as `s i`, kept opaque so
1066	the spanning-element rewrite in `dDiff_exact` has a type-correct motive (the index
1067	must not syntactically contain `↑ρ`). -/
1068	private noncomputable def spanIdx (ρ : ↑(Set.range s)) : ι := ρ.2.choose
1069	
1070	omit [CommRing R] in
1071	private lemma spanIdx_spec (ρ : ↑(Set.range s)) : s (spanIdx s ρ) = ↑ρ := ρ.2.choose_spec
1072	
1073	/-- **Step (a): positive-degree exactness of the un-localised section Čech module
1074	complex `D•`** (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`, the `R`-module core).  For a spanning
1075	family `s : ι → R` (`Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤`), the complex
1076	`∏_σ M_{s_σ}` is exact in positive degrees.  Reduced — via
1077	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` localising at each spanning element `s_r` — to the
1078	exactness `locDiff_exact` of the localised complexes (which is the dependent
1079	combinatorial core `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`), through the localised-
1080	differential identification `map_dDiff_eq_locDiff`. -/
1081	lemma dDiff_exact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (m : ℕ) :
1082	    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) := by
1083	  classical
1084	  haveI inst1 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1085	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1)) := fun ρ => by
1086	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1087	  haveI inst2 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1088	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2)) := fun ρ => by
1089	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1090	  haveI inst3 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1091	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 3)) := fun ρ => by
1092	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1093	  refine exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs
1094	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1095	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1))
1096	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1097	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2))
1098	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 3) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1099	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 3))
1100	    (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) fun ρ => ?_
1101	  rw [map_dDiff_eq_locDiff s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1),
1102	    map_dDiff_eq_locDiff s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2)]
1103	  exact locDiff_exact s M (spanIdx s ρ) m
1104	
1105	end SectionCechModule
1106	
1107	end AlgebraicGeometry
1108

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImage
8	
9	/-!
10	# Čech computation of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (unconditional)
11	
12	This file constructs the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`
13	**without appealing to injective resolutions** in the category of sheaves of
14	modules. The companion `Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean` defines `Rⁱ f_*` as a
15	right derived functor, which requires the ambient category of `O_X`-modules to
16	have enough injectives — a property not currently available for sheaves of
17	modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies over the site. The
18	Čech approach developed here sidesteps the issue: it computes `Rⁱ f_* F` as the
19	cohomology of an explicit complex built from the pushforwards of `F` over the
20	finite intersections of an affine open cover, producing an **unconditional**
21	construction of `Rⁱ f_*` for quasi-coherent `F` and separated quasi-compact `f`.
22	
23	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a quasi-compact, separated morphism of schemes (so
24	all finite intersections of an affine open cover of `X` are again affine), and
25	`F : X.Modules` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. A base change of `f` along
26	`g : S' ⟶ S` is recorded by a cartesian square
27	```
28	  X' --g'--> X
29	  |f'        |f
30	  v          v
31	  S' --g---> S
32	```
33	with `F' = (g')^* F` the pullback of `F` to `X'`.
34	
35	The six main declarations are:
36	
37	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve` — the (augmented) Čech nerve of an affine open
38	  cover, an augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules.
39	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex` — the relative Čech complex in `QCoh(S)`, a
40	  cochain complex of `O_S`-modules whose degree-`p` term is the product of the
41	  pushforwards of `F` over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections of the cover.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine` — Čech acyclicity on affines: the Čech
43	  complex of a standard cover of an affine scheme has vanishing cohomology in
44	  all positive degrees (Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
45	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` — the cohomology of the
46	  relative Čech complex is canonically isomorphic to `Rⁱ f_* F` wherever the
47	  derived functor is defined.
48	
49	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`.
50	
51	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech cohomology of quasi-coherent
52	sheaves and §Quasi-coherence of higher direct images; Tags 02KE
53	(`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent`), 02KG
54	(`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`).
55	-/
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	open Scheme.Modules
64	
65	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
66	
67	/- The Čech nerve `CechNerve` is *constructed* (no longer a hole) further below, once the
68	geometric backbone (`coverCechNerveOverAug`) and the push–pull functor (`pushPullFunctor`)
69	are in scope; see its definition just before `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. -/
70	
71	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scheme-level Čech nerve backbone
72	
73	The genuine construction of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve` factors through two
74	ingredients that are independent of one another:
75	
76	* a *geometric* backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover, an
77	  augmented simplicial **scheme** over `X` (the iterated fibre powers of
78	  `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`), which exists unconditionally because `Scheme` has all
79	  finite limits; and
80	* a *push-pull* functor `(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`, that
81	  turns the simplicial scheme over `X` into the cosimplicial `O_X`-module
82	  `CechNerve`.
83	
84	The backbone (`coverArrow`, `coverCechNerve`) is built here axiom-clean. The
85	push-pull functor is the remaining gap: its `map_comp` requires the
86	`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence isomorphisms (the same coherence
87	quagmire active in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`), so `CechNerve` itself is
88	left as the single genuine hole.
89	
90	Independently of the nerve, the passage *from* an augmented cosimplicial
91	`O_X`-module *to* the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` is pure,
92	coherence-free plumbing (`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`): forget the augmentation,
93	push forward along `f` via `CosimplicialObject.whiskering`, and take the
94	alternating coface-map cochain complex. We record it here so that `CechComplex`
95	is *defined* in terms of `CechNerve` — closing `CechNerve` axiom-clean
96	immediately yields an axiom-clean `CechComplex`. -/
97	
98	/-- The arrow `∐ᵢ Uᵢ ⟶ X` (`Sigma.desc 𝒰.f`) attached to an open cover `𝒰` of a
99	scheme `X`. Its augmented {\v C}ech nerve is the geometric backbone of the
100	relative {\v C}ech complex. Project-local: packages the cover as a single arrow
101	so the existing `Arrow.augmentedCechNerve` machinery applies. -/
102	noncomputable def coverArrow (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : Arrow Scheme.{u} :=
103	  Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)
104	
105	/-- The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an open cover `𝒰`, as an augmented
106	simplicial scheme over `X`: in simplicial degree `p` it is the `(p+1)`-fold
107	fibre power of `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`, i.e. `∐_{(i₀,…,i_p)} U_{i₀} ×ₓ ⋯ ×ₓ U_{i_p}`,
108	with augmentation the cover map to `X`. Exists unconditionally because `Scheme`
109	has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks used by
110	`Arrow.augmentedCechNerve`). Project-local geometric backbone for `CechNerve`. -/
111	noncomputable def coverCechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
112	    SimplicialObject.Augmented Scheme.{u} :=
113	  (coverArrow 𝒰).augmentedCechNerve
114	
115	/-! ### Push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules` — object and morphism bricks
116	
117	The geometric backbone above is lifted to a cosimplicial `O_X`-module by the
118	*relative-direct-image functor on the over-category*
119	```
120	  G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules,   (Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F,
121	```
122	sending an `X`-scheme `p : Y ⟶ X` to the pushforward along `p` of the pullback
123	`p^* F`. We record here the two *pre-coherence* bricks of `G` — its action on
124	objects (`pushPullObj`, the planner's `Gobj`) and on morphisms (`pushPullMap`,
125	the planner's `Gmap`) — both axiom-clean and free of any functor law. The functor
126	laws `G(𝟙) = 𝟙` and `G(g ≫ h) = G(h) ≫ G(g)` are a *consumer* of the
127	pushforward/pullback composition coherence (`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` and
128	their unitor/pentagon identities) and are deferred; see the note after
129	`pushPullMap`. -/
130	
131	/-- The object map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`,
132	`(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. Sends an `X`-scheme `Y` (with structure map `Y.hom : Y.left
133	⟶ X`) to the pushforward along `Y.hom` of the pullback of `F`. Project-local
134	object brick of the {\v C}ech push–pull functor (the planner's `Gobj`). -/
135	noncomputable def pushPullObj (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) : X.Modules :=
136	  (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
137	
138	/-- The morphism map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. For a
139	morphism `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁` of `X`-schemes (so `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`, the
140	over-triangle `Over.w g`), the contravariant functor produces the restriction
141	comparison `pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂`. It is the three-step composite:
142	the unit `η` of the adjunction `pullback g.left ⊣ pushforward g.left`, the
143	pushforward comparison `(pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, and the pushforward
144	of the pullback comparison `(pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, glued by two
145	`eqToHom` transports along the over-triangle `Over.w g`. No functor law is used:
146	this is a reusable pre-coherence brick (the planner's `Gmap`). -/
147	noncomputable def pushPullMap (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
148	    pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂ :=
149	  (pushforward Y₁.hom).map
150	      ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g.left).unit.app
151	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
152	    (pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app
153	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
154	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward q).obj
155	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)))
156	      (Over.w g)) ≫
157	    (pushforward Y₂.hom).map ((pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app F) ≫
158	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward Y₂.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
159	      (Over.w g))
160	
161	/- **The functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`.**
162	Assembling `pushPullObj` / `pushPullMap` into the functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤
163	X.Modules` requires
164	```
165	  pushPullMap_id   : pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y)
166	  pushPullMap_comp : pushPullMap F (g ≫ h) = pushPullMap F h ≫ pushPullMap F g
167	```
168	Both laws are proved axiom-clean below (see `pushPullMap_id` and `pushPullMap_comp`),
169	and `pushPullFunctor` is assembled from them immediately after. -/
170	
171	/-! ### Functor laws of the push–pull functor `G` -/
172	
173	/-- Identity law of the push–pull functor `G`. -/
174	lemma pushPullMap_id (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) :
175	    pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y) := by
176	  -- `star`: the unit-triangle for the identity adjunction `pullback 𝟙 ⊣ pushforward 𝟙`.
177	  have star := unit_conjugateEquiv (Adjunction.id (C := Y.left.Modules))
178	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left))
179	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
180	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom] at star
181	  simp only [Adjunction.id_unit, NatTrans.id_app, Functor.id_obj] at star
182	  -- `hru`: right-unitality of the pullback pseudofunctor, applied at `F`.
183	  have hru := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality (X := Y.left) (f := Y.hom)
184	  have hru2 := congrArg (fun t => NatTrans.app t F) hru
185	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.rightUnitor_hom_app] at hru2
186	  have hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app
187	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) ≫
188	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
189	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)))
190	        (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
191	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.app
192	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F))) := by
193	    apply Scheme.Modules.hom_ext
194	    intro U; rfl
195	  -- the unit zig-zag for the identity adjunction collapses on `M`
196	  have hzig : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left)).unit.app
197	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) ≫
198	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.app
199	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) ≫
200	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) =
201	      𝟙 ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) := by
202	    have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.naturality
203	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F))
204	    simp only [Functor.id_map] at hnat
205	    erw [← hnat, ← reassoc_of% star]
206	    exact Iso.inv_hom_id_app _ _
207	  -- the pullback comparison + the over-triangle transport collapse via right-unitality
208	  have hib_inner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app F ≫
209	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
210	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) := by
211	    rw [eqToHom_app] at hru2
212	    rw [← hru2, ← Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app]; simp
213	  have hib : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
214	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app F) ≫
215	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).obj
216	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
217	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
218	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) := by
219	    have he : eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).obj
220	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
221	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
222	          (eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
223	            (Category.id_comp Y.hom))) := by
224	      rw [eqToHom_map]
225	    rw [he, ← Functor.map_comp]; exact congrArg _ hib_inner
226	  -- assemble
227	  simp only [pushPullMap, Over.id_left]
228	  erw [reassoc_of% hpf, hib, ← Functor.map_comp]
229	  erw [hzig, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl

232	/-- **Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push–pull head.**
233	For composable scheme morphisms `f : A ⟶ B`, `p : B ⟶ Z` and `N : Z.Modules`, the
234	adjunction unit at `N` for `p` followed by the *head* of `pushPullMap` (the
235	pushforward of the unit for `f`, then the pushforward comparison) equals the unit
236	for the composite `f ≫ p` followed by the pushforward of the inverse pullback
237	comparison. This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
238	`η^{f≫p}` into the iterated units `η^p`, `η^f`; it is the reusable ingredient that
239	the functoriality (pentagon) law of `pushPullMap` repeatedly consumes when
240	splitting a composite unit. Project-local supplement. -/
241	lemma pushPull_unit_mate {A B Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : A ⟶ B) (p : B ⟶ Z)
242	    (N : Z.Modules) :
243	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).unit.app N ≫
244	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
245	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
246	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
247	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f p).hom.app
248	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) =
249	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p)).unit.app N ≫
250	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f ≫ p)).map
251	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).inv.app N) := by
252	  have key := unit_conjugateEquiv
253	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).comp
254	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
255	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p))
256	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).inv N
257	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv, Adjunction.comp_unit_app] at key
258	  simpa only [Category.assoc] using key
259	
260	/-- **Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push–pull tail** (kernel-cheap
261	generalised form). The morphism map `pushPullMap` glues its pullback-comparison leg
262	to the target object `pushPullObj F Y₂` by two `eqToHom` coercions along the
263	over-triangle `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`. Cancelling those coercions *in situ*
264	(at the concrete pushforward/pullback objects) provokes a kernel `whnf` blow-up.
265	This lemma states the cancellation **with the over-triangle equality as a free
266	hypothesis** `h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂`, so the proof is a single `subst h` (after which
267	the transports become `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish — kernel-cheap) followed by
268	`simp`. Applying it to `pushPullMap` via `rw` rewrites the tail without forcing the
269	kernel to unfold the comparison objects: the over-triangle leg
270	`eqToHom ≫ (pushforward p₂).map (pullbackComp).hom ≫ eqToHom` collapses to the
271	transport-light `(pushforward (gl ≫ p₁)).map (pullbackComp).hom ≫ eqToHom`, the
272	single residual `eqToHom` carrying the unavoidable object identification of the
273	codomain `pushPullObj F Y₂`. Reusable pre-coherence brick for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
274	lemma pushPull_transport_cancel {Y₁ Y₂ : Scheme.{u}}
275	    (gl : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (p₁ : Y₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Y₂ ⟶ X)
276	    (h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
277	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
278	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gl).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))) h) ≫
279	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gl p₁).hom.app F) ≫
280	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj
281	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) h) =
282	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (gl ≫ p₁)).map
283	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gl p₁).hom.app F) ≫
284	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
285	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) h) := by
286	  subst h
287	  simp

289	/-- **Composite-unit decomposition for the push–pull head.** The adjunction unit
290	`η^{f≫p}` for a composite morphism, expressed through the iterated units `η^p`,
291	`η^f` and the pushforward/pullback comparison isomorphisms. This is the
292	`pushPull_unit_mate` identity solved for `η^{f≫p}` (post-composing with
293	`(f≫p)_*(pullbackComp).hom` cancels the `pullbackComp.inv` factor). Reusable brick
294	for the composition law of `pushPullMap`. -/
295	lemma pushPull_unit_comp {A B Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : A ⟶ B) (p : B ⟶ Z)
296	    (N : Z.Modules) :
297	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p)).unit.app N =
298	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).unit.app N ≫
299	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
300	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
301	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
302	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f p).hom.app
303	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
304	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f ≫ p)).map
305	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).hom.app N) := by
306	  have m := pushPull_unit_mate f p N
307	  erw [reassoc_of% m, ← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app,
308	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]

310	/-- The pushforward pseudofunctor is *strict* on sheaves of modules: the
311	`pushforwardComp` comparison `2`-cell is the identity on the nose. Holds by `rfl`
312	(`pushforward (a ≫ p) = pushforward a ⋙ pushforward p` definitionally). Project-local
313	collapse used to discharge the pushforward legs of the push–pull pentagon. -/
314	lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_id {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p : Z₁ ⟶ Z₃)
315	    (M : Z₂.Modules) : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a p).hom.app M = 𝟙 _ :=
316	  rfl

318	/-- **Scheme-level push–pull comparison map with the over-triangle as a free
319	hypothesis.** This is the body of `pushPullMap` with the underlying scheme map
320	`a`, the two structure maps `p₁ p₂`, and the over-triangle `w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂`
321	generalised away from the `Over X` packaging. Spelling the over-triangle as a free
322	hypothesis is what makes the composition law provable by `subst` (after which the
323	`eqToHom` over-triangle transports become `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish, dodging the
324	kernel `whnf` wall). By construction `pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom
325	Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F` definitionally. Project-local. -/
326	noncomputable def rawPushPullMap {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁)
327	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
328	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ⟶
329	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₂).obj F) :=
330	  (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
331	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
332	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
333	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a p₁).hom.app
334	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
335	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
336	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))) w) ≫
337	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
338	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj
339	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) w)

342	/-- `pushPullMap` is the `Over X`-instance of `rawPushPullMap`. Holds by `rfl`. -/
343	lemma pushPullMap_eq_raw (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
344	    pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F :=
345	  rfl

347	/- **Composition law `pushPullMap_comp` — reduced to an explicit clean pentagon, not
348	yet closed.** The kernel `whnf` wall that blocked iters 264/265/271 is fully removed:
349	the route is `pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F`
350	(`pushPullMap_eq_raw`, by `rfl`), reducing `pushPullMap_comp` to
351	`rawPushPullMap_comp`, which has the two over-triangles `wg : a ≫ p₁ = p₂`,
352	`wh : b ≫ p₂ = p₃` as **free hypotheses**. `subst wg; subst wh` then makes every
353	`eqToHom` over-triangle transport `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish (kernel-cheap — no
354	`whnf` blow-up). After `simp only [rawPushPullMap, eqToHom_refl]`,
355	`rw [pushPull_unit_comp b a _]` (the composite-unit decomposition),
356	`simp only [pushforwardComp_hom_app_id]` (pushforward is *strict*: the comparison
357	2-cells are `𝟙` by `rfl`) and absorbing the resulting defeq-`𝟙`s by
358	`repeat erw [Category.id_comp]`, the goal becomes the genuine **pullback
359	pseudofunctor pentagon** (no transports, no `pushforwardComp`):
360	
361	  `(pushforward p₁).map (a_*(η^b) ≫ (b≫a)_*(pullbackComp b a).hom.app(p₁^*F)) ≫`
362	  `  (pushforward (b≫a≫p₁)).map ((pullbackComp (b≫a) p₁).hom.app F) ≫ eqToHom`
363	  ` = (pushforward (a≫p₁)).map (η^b ≫ (pullback b ⋙ pushforward b).map((pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) ≫`
364	  `  (pushforward (b≫a≫p₁)).map ((pullbackComp b (a≫p₁)).hom.app F)`
365	
366	where the leading `(pushforward p₁).map (η^a)` has already been peeled and the `η^b`
367	units aligned by `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.naturality`. The residual
368	content is `Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)`
369	(its four `pullbackComp` 2-cells `pullbackComp b a`, `pullbackComp (b≫a) p₁`,
370	`pullbackComp a p₁`, `pullbackComp b (a≫p₁)` match exactly) together with the single
371	associativity cell `eqToHom ((b≫a)≫p₁ = b≫(a≫p₁))`.
372	
373	**Why it is not yet closed (next-prover dead-ends, all hit this iter):** the regime is
374	pervasively *defeq-not-syntactic*. `rw`/`reassoc_of%` of `Category.assoc`,
375	`Functor.map_comp`, the lead `rfl`-bridge, and `pseudofunctor_associativity` all fail
376	to match visually-identical terms (the pushforward/`SheafOfModules` `≫` and `.map`
377	carry non-syntactic instances). `erw` matches but **whnf-unfolds `pullbackComp` into
378	its raw `TwoSquare.equivNatTrans`/`mateEquiv` mate definition**, exploding the goal.
379	`congr 1` on the defeq-but-not-syntactic leading factors recurses into the functor
380	structure and likewise unfolds `pullbackComp` into HEq goals. The promising next route
381	is to reformulate via the adjunction transpose (`(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _).homEquiv`)
382	so the pentagon becomes a `pullback`-side identity provable by `pseudofunctor_associativity`
383	without touching the strict-pushforward defeq wall, OR to add a strictness-aware
384	`@[simp]` normal form for `pushforward (a≫b)` so the lead peel is syntactic. The bricks
385	`rawPushPullMap`, `pushPullMap_eq_raw`, `pushPull_unit_comp`, `pushforwardComp_hom_app_id`
386	below are the axiom-clean scaffolding for that final step. -/
387	
388	/-- Clean (transport-free) form of `rawPushPullMap` when the over-triangle is `rfl`
389	(`p₂ = a ≫ p₁`): the pushforward comparison `pushforwardComp` and the `eqToHom`
390	coercions all collapse, leaving `(pushforward p₁).map` of the mate head
391	`η^a ≫ a_*(pullbackComp a p₁).hom`. Project-local helper for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
392	lemma rawPushPullMap_self {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X)
393	    (F : X.Modules) :
394	    rawPushPullMap a p₁ (a ≫ p₁) rfl F =
395	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
396	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
397	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
398	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
399	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) := by
400	  simp only [rawPushPullMap, pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp,
401	    Category.comp_id, Functor.map_comp]
402	  rfl

405	/-- Clean form of `rawPushPullMap` for a general over-triangle `w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂`:
406	the transport-free head `(pushforward p₁).map (η^a ≫ a_*(pullbackComp a p₁).hom)`
407	followed by the single `eqToHom` identifying the target along `w`. Project-local
408	helper for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
409	lemma rawPushPullMap_self_gen {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X)
410	    (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
411	    rawPushPullMap a p₁ p₂ w F =
412	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
413	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
414	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
415	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
416	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) ≫
417	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
418	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) w) := by
419	  subst w
420	  rw [rawPushPullMap_self]
421	  exact (Category.comp_id _).symm

423	/-- The pure pullback **pentagon** at `F`, in transport-light form: the content of
424	`pushPullMap_comp` once the units and pushforwards are peeled. It is exactly
425	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)` evaluated at
426	`F`, with the associator (an identity on components) absorbed and the two leading
427	comparison isos inverted. Project-local. -/
428	lemma pushPull_pentagon {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (b : Z₃ ⟶ Z₂)
429	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (F : X.Modules) :
430	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
431	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F ≫
432	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
433	          (Category.assoc b a p₁)) =
434	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
435	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F := by
436	  have H := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)
437	  have HF := congrArg (fun t => NatTrans.app t F) H
438	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
439	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app, Functor.comp_obj,
440	    Category.id_comp] at HF
441	  -- HF : A1⁻¹ ≫ B1⁻¹ ≫ C ≫ D = eqToHom eF, with the associator (= 𝟙) absorbed.
442	  -- Cancel the two leading isos `B1 = (pullback b).map (pullbackComp a p₁).hom`, `A1 = pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)`
443	  -- against their inverses (no `IsIso` instances needed: `Iso.hom_inv_id_app` + functoriality),
444	  -- then feed `HF` in via `congrArg`/`trans` (defeq, so no fragile `rw [HF]` matching).
445	  have cancel : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
446	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫
447	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).inv.app F ≫
448	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).inv.app F) ≫
449	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
450	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F =
451	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
452	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F := by
453	    have h1 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫
454	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).inv.app F = 𝟙 _ := Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _
455	    have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
456	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).inv.app F) = 𝟙 _ :=
457	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
458	        ((congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _)).trans
459	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_id _))
460	    simp only [Functor.comp_obj] at h1 h2 ⊢
461	    rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]
462	  have hcd := cancel.symm.trans (congrArg (fun t => (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
463	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
464	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫ t) HF)
465	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app
466	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))
467	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F), hcd]
468	  simp [eqToHom_trans]
