471	/-- Composition law for `rawPushPullMap` with the two over-triangles as free
472	hypotheses (kernel-cheap). -/
473	lemma rawPushPullMap_comp {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (b : Z₃ ⟶ Z₂)
474	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (p₃ : Z₃ ⟶ X)
475	    (wg : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (wh : b ≫ p₂ = p₃) (F : X.Modules) :
476	    rawPushPullMap (b ≫ a) p₁ p₃ (by rw [Category.assoc, wg, wh]) F =
477	      rawPushPullMap a p₁ p₂ wg F ≫ rawPushPullMap b p₂ p₃ wh F := by
478	  subst wg wh
479	  rw [rawPushPullMap_self a p₁ F, rawPushPullMap_self b (a ≫ p₁) F,
480	      rawPushPullMap_self_gen (b ≫ a) p₁ (b ≫ a ≫ p₁) (Category.assoc b a p₁) F]
481	  -- The over-triangle `eqToHom` (in `X.Modules`) is `(pushforward p₁).map` of the
482	  -- corresponding `Z₁.Modules`-level `eqToHom` (`pushforward` is strict).
483	  have he : eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
484	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.assoc b a p₁)) =
485	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map (eqToHom (congrArg
486	        (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (b ≫ a)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
487	        (Category.assoc b a p₁))) := by
488	    rw [eqToHom_map]; rfl
489	  -- The inner identity in `Z₁.Modules`: the pure pushforward-of-pentagon content.
490	  have INNER : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a)).unit.app
491	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
492	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (b ≫ a)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F) ≫
493	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (b ≫ a)).obj
494	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.assoc b a p₁)) =
495	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
496	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
497	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) ≫
498	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
499	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
500	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) ≫
501	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
502	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F)) := by
503	    rw [pushPull_unit_comp b a ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)]
504	    -- The `Z₂.Modules`-level content: the pushforward-`b` of the pullback pentagon, with the
505	    -- composite unit straightened by naturality of `η^b`.
506	    have INNER2 :
507	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
508	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
509	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
510	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
511	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F ≫
512	              eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
513	                (Category.assoc b a p₁))) =
514	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F ≫
515	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
516	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) ≫
517	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
518	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F) := by
519	      have key2 : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
520	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
521	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F ≫
522	              eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
523	                (Category.assoc b a p₁))) =
524	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
525	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
526	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F) :=
527	        congrArg _ (pushPull_pentagon a b p₁ F)
528	      have nat2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
529	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
530	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
531	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) =
532	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F ≫
533	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
534	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) :=
535	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.naturality
536	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)).symm
537	      refine (congrArg (fun t => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
538	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫ t) key2).trans ?_
539	      rw [Functor.map_comp]
540	      exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
541	        ((congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
542	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F)) nat2).trans (Category.assoc _ _ _))
543	    exact congrArg (fun t => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
544	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map t) INNER2
545	  -- Expose the second RHS factor as `(pushforward p₁).map (…)` (strictness, by `rfl`) so the
546	  -- `map_comp` unifications below stay kernel-cheap.
547	  rw [show (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ p₁)).map
548	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
549	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) ≫
550	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F)) =
551	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
552	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
553	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) ≫
554	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F)))
555	        from rfl]
556	  convert congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map INNER using 2
557	
558	/-- **Push–pull functor `G` — composition law** (contravariant functoriality).
559	For composable morphisms `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁`, `h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂` of `X`-schemes,
560	`G(h ≫ g) = G(g) ≫ G(h)` (writing `≫` for `Over X`-composition; in the informal
561	`∘`-notation of the blueprint this is `G(g ∘ h) = G(h) ∘ G(g)`). Together with
562	`pushPullMap_id` this assembles `pushPullObj`/`pushPullMap` into a genuine functor
563	`(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp`). -/
564	lemma pushPullMap_comp (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂) :
565	    pushPullMap F (h ≫ g) = pushPullMap F g ≫ pushPullMap F h := by
566	  rw [pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw]
567	  exact rawPushPullMap_comp g.left h.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom Y₃.hom (Over.w g) (Over.w h) F
568	
569	/-- **The push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`**, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`.
570	Assembled from the object brick `pushPullObj`, the morphism brick `pushPullMap`, and
571	the two functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`. Contravariant on `Over X`
572	(hence a covariant functor out of `(Over X)ᵒᵖ`): a morphism `φ : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂` in
573	`(Over X)ᵒᵖ` is `φ.unop : Y₂.unop ⟶ Y₁.unop` in `Over X`, sent to
574	`pushPullMap F φ.unop`. This is the functor the planner calls `G`; composing the
575	geometric Čech backbone `coverCechNerve` with it produces the cosimplicial
576	`O_X`-module nerve `CechNerve`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp` consumer). -/
577	noncomputable def pushPullFunctor (F : X.Modules) : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules where
578	  obj Y := pushPullObj F Y.unop
579	  map φ := pushPullMap F φ.unop
580	  map_id Y := pushPullMap_id F Y.unop
581	  map_comp φ ψ := pushPullMap_comp F φ.unop ψ.unop
582	
583	/-- The geometric Čech backbone, lifted to a **simplicial object in `Over X`**: each
584	fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
585	and the face/degeneracy maps are `X`-morphisms by naturality of the augmentation into
586	the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
587	`CategoryTheory.Over.lift` applied to the augmentation natural transformation. -/
588	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
589	  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom
590	
591	/-- The over-category Čech backbone as an **augmented** simplicial object in `Over X`:
592	`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
593	augmentation map at each simplicial level is the unique morphism to the terminal object
594	(its underlying scheme map is the level's structure map to `X`), and the augmentation
595	coherence condition holds automatically because the augmentation target is terminal.
596	Project-local. -/
597	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
598	    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
599	  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
600	    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
601	    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)
602	
603	/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
604	backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
605	push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
606	of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
607	noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
608	    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
609	  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F
610	
611	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
612	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
613	
614	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
615	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
616	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
617	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
618	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
619	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
620	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
621	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
622	
623	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
624	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
625	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
626	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
627	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
628	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
629	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
630	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
631	are what make this assembly legitimate.
632	
633	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
634	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
635	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
636	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
637	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
638	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
639	
640	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
641	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
642	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
643	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
644	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
645	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
646	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
647	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
648	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
649	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
650	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
651	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
652	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
653	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
654	
655	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
656	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
657	
658	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
659	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
660	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
661	```
662	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
663	```
664	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
665	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
666	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
667	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
668	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
669	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
670	quasi-coherent.
671	
672	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
673	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
674	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
675	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
676	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
677	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
678	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
679	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
680	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
681	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
682	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)
683	
684	/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
685	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
686	`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).
687	
688	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
689	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
690	every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
691	complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
692	isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
693	```
694	  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.
695	```
696	In particular, over an affine base `S = Spec A`, taking global sections gives
697	`Hⁱ(X, F) = Čⁱ(𝒰, F) = H⁰(S, Rⁱ f_* F)` as `A`-modules.
698	
699	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` and compare against the
700	derived-functor higher direct image `higherDirectImage` wherever the latter is
701	defined (`HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules`).
702	
703	Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the augmented Čech complex is a termwise
704	`f_*`-acyclic resolution of `F` (each term is a product of pushforwards over
705	affine intersections, acyclic by `CechAcyclic.affine` via the P3b bridge), so
706	the P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) gives
707	`Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`. -/
708	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
709	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
710	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
711	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
712	  -- Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the Čech complex is a termwise acyclic
713	  -- resolution of `F` (by `CechAcyclic.affine`), so `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`
714	  -- gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`.
715	  sorry
716	
717	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` of quasi-coherent sheaves (`i ≥ 1`)
10	
11	This file treats the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`,
12	complementing the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean`.
13	
14	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of
15	modules on `X`. The `i`-th higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` is the `i`-th right
16	derived functor of the pushforward functor `f_*` applied to `F`.
17	
18	This file supplies the single declaration needed downstream by the Čech
19	computation of higher direct images:
20	
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage` — the higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F`,
22	  the derived-functor object that `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` compares the
23	  Čech complex against.
24	
25	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_HigherDirectImage.tex`.
26	
27	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Definition of `Rⁱ f_*`.
28	-/
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits
33	
34	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
35	
36	open Scheme.Modules
37	
38	variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}
39	
40	/-- The `i`-th higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` of a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`
41	along a morphism `f : X ⟶ S`, defined as the `i`-th right derived functor of the
42	pushforward functor `f_*` applied to `F`.
43	
44	For `i = 0` this recovers the ordinary pushforward `R⁰ f_* F = f_* F`.
45	
46	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Definition of `Rⁱ f_*`. -/
47	noncomputable def higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
48	    (f : X ⟶ S) (i : ℕ) (F : X.Modules) : S.Modules :=
49	  ((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F
50	
51	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImage
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
9	
10	/-!
11	# Presheaf description of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (Stacks 01XJ)
12	
13	This file builds project-local infrastructure towards
14	`lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`: the higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` is the
15	sheafification of the presheaf `V ↦ Hⁱ(f⁻¹(V), F)`.
16	
17	The categorical heart of Stacks 01XJ is that **cohomology of a complex of sheaves
18	of modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology**:
19	this holds because the sheafification functor of presheaves of modules is exact
20	(it preserves finite limits — `Mathlib`'s
21	`PresheafOfModules.instPreservesFiniteLimitsSheafification` — and, being a left
22	adjoint, finite colimits), hence preserves homology, and the counit of the
23	sheafification adjunction is an isomorphism on sheaves.
24	
25	This file isolates that engine (`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`) and feeds
26	it through the right-derived-functor computation
27	(`InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`) to express `Rⁿ f_* G`, for a chosen
28	injective resolution of `G`, as the sheafification of the presheaf-level homology
29	of the pushed-forward resolution
30	(`AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology`). The
31	remaining step to the blueprint statement — identifying that presheaf-level
32	homology with the cohomology presheaf `V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` — is handed off (see
33	the `## Why I stopped` note in the task result).
34	
35	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`
36	(`lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`, `def:higher_direct_image`).
37	-/
38	
39	universe v u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits
42	
43	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — homology of a complex under a functor -/
44	
45	namespace CategoryTheory.Functor
46	
47	/-- The homological-complex level of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`: an additive,
48	homology-preserving functor `F` commutes with taking homology of a complex,
49	`((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).homology i ≅ F.obj (K.homology i)`.
50	
51	Project-local: `Mathlib` provides this isomorphism only at the `ShortComplex`
52	level (`ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`); we lift it degreewise to
53	`HomologicalComplex` (the two `homology` objects are definitionally the homology
54	of the relevant short complexes, so the lift is the `ShortComplex` isomorphism
55	applied to `K.sc i`). -/
56	noncomputable def mapHomologyIso'
57	    {A B : Type*} [Category A] [Category B] [Abelian A] [Abelian B]
58	    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]
59	    {ι : Type*} (c : ComplexShape ι) (K : HomologicalComplex A c) (i : ι) :
60	    ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).homology i ≅ F.obj (K.homology i) :=
61	  (K.sc i).mapHomologyIso F
62	
63	end CategoryTheory.Functor
64	
65	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheaf homology is sheafified presheaf homology -/
66	
67	namespace PresheafOfModules
68	
69	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
70	  {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{v}} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v}} (α : R₀ ⟶ R.obj)
71	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
72	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
73	  {ιc : Type} (cc : ComplexShape ιc)
74	
75	/-- The sheafification functor of presheaves of modules is additive (it preserves
76	binary products, being a left adjoint that preserves finite limits).
77	
78	Project-local: needed so that `sheafification α` can be applied to homological
79	complexes via `mapHomologicalComplex`. -/
80	noncomputable instance sheafificationAdditive :
81	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).Additive :=
82	  Functor.additive_of_preserves_binary_products _
83	
84	/-- The counit of the sheafification adjunction, applied levelwise to a complex of
85	sheaves of modules, is an isomorphism of complexes
86	`sheafify (forget K) ≅ K`. This is the sheaf-side input to
87	`homologyIsoSheafify`: a sheaf complex is recovered (up to canonical iso) by
88	sheafifying its underlying presheaf complex.
89	
90	Project-local: assembled from `Mathlib`'s `IsIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit`
91	via `isoOfComponents`. -/
92	noncomputable def counitComplexIso (K : HomologicalComplex (SheafOfModules.{v} R) cc) :
93	    ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
94	        (((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
95	          cc).obj K) ≅ K :=
96	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents
97	    (fun n => (asIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit).app (K.X n))
98	    (fun n m _ => ((sheafificationAdjunction α).counit.naturality (K.d n m)).symm)
99	
100	/-- **Engine for Stacks 01XJ.** Homology of a complex of sheaves of modules is the
101	sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of its underlying
102	presheaf complex:
103	`K.homology i ≅ (sheafification α).obj ((forget K).homology i)`.
104	
105	The proof transports homology across the counit isomorphism `sheafify (forget K) ≅ K`
106	of `counitComplexIso` and then uses that `sheafification α` preserves homology
107	(it is exact: preserves finite limits and, as a left adjoint, finite colimits),
108	via `Functor.mapHomologyIso'`.
109	
110	Project-local: `Mathlib` has the exactness ingredients but not this packaged
111	"cohomology sheaf = sheafify of objectwise cohomology" comparison. -/
112	noncomputable def homologyIsoSheafify (K : HomologicalComplex (SheafOfModules.{v} R) cc)
113	    (i : ιc) :
114	    K.homology i ≅ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).obj
115	      ((((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
116	        cc).obj K).homology i) :=
117	  (HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor _ cc i).mapIso (counitComplexIso α cc K).symm ≪≫
118	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).mapHomologyIso' cc
119	      (((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
120	        cc).obj K) i
121	
122	end PresheafOfModules
123	
124	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Rⁿ f_*` as a sheafified presheaf homology -/
125	
126	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
127	
128	variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}
129	
130	/-- The underlying presheaf-of-modules complex of the pushed-forward injective
131	resolution `f_* I^•`, whose `n`-th objectwise homology is the cohomology presheaf
132	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` (with the implicit choice of injective resolution `I`).
133	
134	Project-local: the presheaf whose sheafification is `Rⁿ f_* G`. -/
135	noncomputable def pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex
136	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] (f : X ⟶ S) {G : X.Modules}
137	    (I : InjectiveResolution G) :
138	    HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf.obj) (ComplexShape.up ℕ) :=
139	  ((SheafOfModules.forget S.ringCatSheaf ⋙
140	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapHomologicalComplex
141	        (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
142	    (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj I.cocomplex)
143	
144	/-- **Stacks 01XJ, resolution form.** For any chosen injective resolution
145	`I : InjectiveResolution G`, the `n`-th higher direct image `Rⁿ f_* G` is the
146	sheafification of the presheaf-level homology of the pushed-forward resolution
147	`f_* I^•`. Concretely, `Rⁿ f_* G ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hⁿ((f_* I^•)(V)))`, and the
148	presheaf `V ↦ Hⁿ((f_* I^•)(V)) = Hⁿ(I^•(f⁻¹ V))` is the cohomology presheaf
149	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` of the blueprint statement.
150	
151	The proof composes `InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj` (computing the right
152	derived functor `Rⁿ f_* = (f_*).rightDerived n` via the resolution) with the
153	sheafification engine `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`.
154	
155	Project-local: the resolution-dependent form of `lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`;
156	the identification of the displayed presheaf with the absolute cohomology presheaf
157	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` is the remaining hand-off step. -/
158	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology
159	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] (f : X ⟶ S) (n : ℕ) {G : X.Modules}
160	    (I : InjectiveResolution G) :
161	    higherDirectImage f n G ≅
162	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
163	        ((pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex f I).homology n) :=
164	  I.isoRightDerivedObj (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f) n ≪≫
165	    PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj) (ComplexShape.up ℕ)
166	      (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
167	        I.cocomplex) n
168	
169	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Presheaf-level Čech section complex (P3b)
11	
12	This file contains the section Čech complex and the free-Yoneda equivalences:
13	- `sectionCechComplex`        — cosimplicial `Ab`-valued section Čech complex
14	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex}`)
15	- `freeYonedaHomEquiv`        — Hom-set equivalence out of the free presheaf
16	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomEquiv}`)
17	- `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`     — additive version of the above
18	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv}`)
19	- `injective_toPresheafOfModules` — injectivity transport to presheaves of modules
20	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.injective_toPresheafOfModules}`)
21	
22	Related machinery that has moved to sibling files:
23	- `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` → `FreePresheafComplex.lean`
24	- `cechComplex_hom_identification` / `injective_cech_acyclic` → `CechBridge.lean`
25	-/
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-
34	Planner strategy (P3b presheaf-Čech bridge; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
35	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
36	
37	The goal is to prove that injective `O_X`-modules have trivial positive-degree Čech
38	cohomology with respect to any open cover.  The strategy avoids `presheafModules_
39	enoughInjectives` and `cech_delta_functor_presheaves` (decisions 4–5 in the analogy
40	file are expensive Mathlib gaps and are NOT on the critical path).  The build order is:
41	
42	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	Step 1 — `sectionCechComplex`
44	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
45	Type: given a scheme `X`, an open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, an open `U : Opens ↥X`,
46	and a presheaf of modules `F : X.PresheafOfModules`, produce the section Čech
47	cochain complex
48	
49	    Č•(𝒰, F, U) : CochainComplex (ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.val.obj (op U))) ℕ
50	
51	whose degree-`p` term is the product `∏_{(i₀,…,iₚ) : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.J}
52	  F.obj (op (𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ ⊓ U))`, equipped with the alternating restriction
53	differential.
54	
55	THIS OBJECT IS DISTINCT from `CechComplex` in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`:
56	`CechComplex` lives in `S.Modules` (pushforward along `f`) and is a relative complex.
57	`sectionCechComplex` is a plain cochain complex of `R`-modules where `R = O_X(U)`.
58	
59	Key Mathlib hooks:
60	- `PresheafOfModules` — the category `X.PresheafOfModules =
61	  PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.val`.
62	- `PresheafOfModules.evaluation R (op V) : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat (R.obj (op V))`
63	  — the exact "sections over `V`" functor; use it to extract `F.obj (op V)`.
64	- `CochainComplex.of` or `HomologicalComplex.mk` — assemble the complex from
65	  degree-`p` objects and differentials.
66	
67	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	Step 2 — `cechFreePresheafComplex`
69	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70	Type: given `X` and `𝒰`, produce the chain complex of presheaves of modules
71	
72	    K(𝒰)_• : ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ
73	
74	whose degree-`p` term is the direct sum `⨁_{(i₀,…,iₚ) : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.J}
75	  (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ))`.
76	
77	DO NOT introduce a bespoke extension-by-zero functor `j_!`.  The blueprint's
78	`(j_{i₀…iₚ})_! (O_X|_{U_{i₀…iₚ}})` is canonically identified with
79	`(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj U_…)`: on sections over `V`, this is
80	the free `R(V)`-module on `Hom(V, U_…)` — which is `R(V)` when `V ⊆ U_…` and `0`
81	otherwise, matching extension-by-zero of `O_X|_{U_…}` exactly.
82	
83	The differentials are `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map` of the representable maps
84	`yoneda.obj (U_{i₀⊓…⊓U_{iₚ₊₁}}) → yoneda.obj (U_{i₀⊓…⊓̂ᵢⱼ⊓…⊓U_{iₚ₊₁}})` induced
85	by the inclusion of opens `𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ₊₁ ≤ 𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓̂ᵢⱼ ⊓ …`, with
86	the standard alternating sign.
87	
88	Key Mathlib hooks:
89	- `PresheafOfModules.free R : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` — the free
90	  presheaf-of-modules functor.
91	- `PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction R : free R ⊣ toPresheaf R ⋙ whiskeringRight … (forget Ab)`
92	  — the adjunction; `freeObjDesc` / `freeHomEquiv` give the universal property
93	  `Hom(free(P), F) ≅ NatTrans P (F ∘ forget)` used in step 3.
94	- `yoneda : Opens ↥X ⥤ (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the Yoneda embedding.
95	- Use `Finset.univ`-indexed direct sums; `ChainComplex.of` or `HomologicalComplex.mk`
96	  to assemble.
97	
98	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
99	Step 3 — `cechComplex_hom_identification`
100	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
101	Type: a natural isomorphism of cochain complexes of abelian groups (or
102	`O_X(U)`-modules):
103	
104	    Hom_{X.PresheafOfModules}(K(𝒰)_•, F)  ≅  Č•(𝒰, F, U)
105	
106	i.e., the complex of hom-sets out of `K(𝒰)_•` is the section Čech cochain complex.
107	
108	Strategy:
109	- At degree `p`, term-by-term: use the adjunction iso
110	  `(PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction _).homEquiv` to identify
111	  `Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda(U_I)), F)  ≅  NatTrans (yoneda(U_I)) (toPresheaf F)`
112	  and then Yoneda (`yonedaEquiv`) to get `≅  (toPresheaf F).obj (op U_I) = F.obj (op U_I)`.
113	  The product over multi-indices matches the degree-`p` term of `Č•(𝒰, F, U)`.
114	- Check that the differential intertwines: the differential on `Hom(K_•, F)` induced
115	  by pre-composition with the alternating-sign `free.map` maps matches the alternating
116	  restriction differential of `Č•(𝒰, F, U)`.
117	- Build as a cochain-complex isomorphism using `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`.
118	
119	Key Mathlib hooks:
120	- `PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction` — the core adjunction.
121	- `yonedaEquiv` — the Yoneda natural bijection.
122	- `PresheafOfModules.evaluation` — sections extraction.
123	
124	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125	Step 4 — `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
126	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	Type: the augmented complex `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰[0]` (augmentation = "sheaf restricted to
128	the cover") is a quasi-isomorphism, i.e., `K(𝒰)_•` is a free resolution of `O_𝒰`.
129	
130	Strategy (Mathlib has no packaged Čech contractibility lemma; build it directly):
131	- Homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise
132	  (limits/colimits in `PresheafOfModules R` are objectwise; see `HasColimits`
133	  instance). So it suffices to show that for each open `V : Opens ↥X`,
134	  the complex `K(𝒰)_•(V)` of `R(V)`-modules is contractible.
135	- The sectionwise complex `K(𝒰)_•(V)` is:
136	  degree `p` = `⨁_{I : Fin(p+1)→J} R(V)` when `V ⊆ U_{I(0)} ⊓ … ⊓ U_{I(p)}`, else `0`.
137	- Fix any index `i_fix` such that `V ⊆ 𝒰.U i_fix` (if no such index, the complex is
138	  `0` and trivially exact). The contracting homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` is:
139	      `h(s)_{i₀,i₁,…,iₚ} = (i₀ = i_fix) · s_{i₁,…,iₚ}`
140	  (extend the multi-index by prepending `i_fix`). Check: `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
141	- Assemble using `Homotopy` / `HomotopyEquiv` from Mathlib (`HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`
142	  in `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`). From the chain homotopy conclude
143	  `HomotopyEquiv` and hence quasi-isomorphism.
144	- Do NOT route through `ExtraDegeneracy` (it has a different index convention and is
145	  not directly applicable here).
146	
147	Key Mathlib hooks:
148	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — chain homotopy type.
149	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — chain homotopy ⟹ quasi-iso.
150	
151	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
152	Step 5 — `injective_cech_acyclic`
153	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
154	Type: for an injective `I : X.Modules` and an open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, the
155	positive-degree Čech cohomology `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for all `p > 0`.
156	
157	Strategy (two independent parts; does NOT need decisions 4–5):
158	
159	Part (a) — Injective sheaf ⟹ injective presheaf:
160	- Apply `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint` to the adjunction
161	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction α` where `α = 𝟙` for the scheme `X`.
162	  This adjunction is `sheafification α ⊣ (forget R) ⋙ restrictScalars α`, i.e.
163	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion `toPresheafOfModules`.
164	- The left adjoint `sheafification` is exact (it is exact as a colimit of
165	  sheafification-endofunctors over a Grothendieck topology), hence in particular
166	  `PreservesMonomorphisms`. So `Injective.injective_of_adjoint` gives:
167	  `I` injective in `X.Modules` ⟹ `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules.obj I`
168	  is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`.
169	- Relevant Mathlib names:
170	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction`,
171	  `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint`,
172	  `Functor.preservesInjectiveObjects_of_adjunction_of_preservesMonomorphisms`,
173	  `Adjunction.map_injective`.
174	
175	Part (b) — Vanishing of positive Čech cohomology:
176	- By step 4, `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰[0]` is an exact augmented complex (quasi-iso from step 4).
177	- Since `I_pshf := toPresheafOfModules.obj I` is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`
178	  (by part (a)), the functor `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` is exact.
179	- Applying `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` to the exact augmented complex `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰` and
180	  using step 3 (`cechComplex_hom_identification`) to identify the resulting complex
181	  with `Č•(𝒰, I, U)`, conclude that `Č•(𝒰, I, U)` is exact in positive degrees, i.e.
182	  `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for `p > 0`.
183	- This is the Stacks-project proof of `lemma-injective-trivial-cech` (Stacks tag
184	  0BKP and surrounding; see `references/stacks-cohomology.md`).
185	
186	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
187	Verified Mathlib API summary (LSP-checked in this project):
188	  PresheafOfModules
189	  PresheafOfModules.free
190	  PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction
191	  PresheafOfModules.evaluation
192	  PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
193	  CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint
194	  yoneda
195	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196	-/
197	
198	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — PresheafCech (injective transfer) -/
199	
200	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
201	
202	/-- **An injective sheaf of `O_X`-modules is injective as a presheaf of modules.**
203	
204	This is Part 1 of the {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injectives (blueprint
205	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`): the inclusion
206	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules` is the right
207	adjoint of the (exact) sheafification functor, so it carries injective objects to
208	injective objects.
209	
210	Project-local because Mathlib has no packaged "injective sheaf of modules ⟹ injective
211	presheaf of modules" statement; it is obtained here by feeding
212	`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 _)` (whose left adjoint
213	`sheafification` preserves monomorphisms) to `Injective.injective_of_adjoint`. The
214	right adjoint of that adjunction is *definitionally* `toPresheafOfModules X`
215	(`SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)`). -/
216	theorem injective_toPresheafOfModules (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] :
217	    Injective ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) := by
218	  -- the left adjoint of `toPresheafOfModules X` is `sheafification (𝟙 _)`, which is
219	  -- exact and in particular preserves monomorphisms
220	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).PreservesMonomorphisms :=
221	    inferInstance
222	  -- realign the ambient category instance so the `Injective` instance is found cheaply
223	  haveI : Injective (C := SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf) I := ‹Injective I›
224	  exact Injective.injective_of_adjoint
225	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) I
226	
227	/-- **Free–Yoneda representability (per-term bijection).**
228	
229	For an open `V : Opens X` and a presheaf of `O_X`-modules `F`, the morphisms out of the
230	free presheaf of modules on the representable `yoneda V` are exactly the sections of
231	`F` over `V`:
232	```
233	  Hom_{PMod(O_X)}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V).
234	```
235	This is the free–forgetful adjunction (`PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv`) composed with
236	the Yoneda bijection (`yonedaEquiv`). It is the single-multi-index core of the
237	{\v C}ech complex hom-identification (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`,
238	Stacks `lemma-cech-map-into`): taking the product over multi-indices turns this into
239	`Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
240	
241	Project-local because Mathlib packages the two halves separately but not their
242	composite as the corepresentability of evaluation by `free(yoneda V)`. The target
243	`(F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (op V)` is the underlying set of the section group
244	`F(V)`. -/
245	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
246	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
247	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃
248	      (F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (Opposite.op V) :=
249	  PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv.trans yonedaEquiv
250	
251	/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
252	`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
253	canonical free generator `freeMk (𝟙 V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
254	description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
255	unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
256	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
257	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
258	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
259	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
260	  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl
261	
262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl
287	
288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# Acyclic resolutions compute right-derived functors
10	
11	This file provides the abstract homological-algebra core underlying the Čech
12	computation of higher direct images (Stacks Tag 015E, Leray's acyclicity lemma):
13	**an acyclic resolution computes every right-derived functor**.
14	
15	Throughout, `𝒜` and `ℬ` are abelian categories, `𝒜` has injective resolutions
16	(so that `G.rightDerived n` is everywhere defined), and `G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ` is an
17	additive functor.
18	
19	## Declarations
20	
21	* `CategoryTheory.Functor.IsRightAcyclic` — typeclass for right-`G`-acyclic objects.
22	* Instance: every injective object is right-acyclic (from Mathlib's
23	  `Functor.isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ`).

25	The following declarations are outlined in the strategy block below and will be
26	constructed by the prover in `mathlib-build` mode:
27	
28	* `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact` — dual Horseshoe Lemma:
29	  lift `0 → A → B → C → 0` to a degreewise-split SES of injective resolutions.
30	* `CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic` — dimension-shift
31	  isomorphism `(Rᵏ G)(Z) ≅ (Rᵏ⁺¹ G)(A)` across a SES with acyclic middle term.
32	* `CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` — main theorem:
33	  `(Rⁿ G)(A) ≅ Hⁿ(G(J•))` for any acyclic resolution `J•` of `A`.
34	
35	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_AcyclicResolution.tex` and
36	`.archon/analogies/p4-derived-les.md` for the full informal argument.

63	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — middle-term quasi-isomorphism transfer
64	
65	Given a morphism `φ : S₁ ⟶ S₂` between two short exact sequences of homological complexes
66	in an abelian category, Mathlib proves that if `φ.τ₁` and `φ.τ₂` are quasi-isomorphisms then so
67	is `φ.τ₃` (`HomologicalComplex.HomologySequence.quasiIso_τ₃`). The companion statements for `φ.τ₁`
68	and `φ.τ₂` are an explicit Mathlib TODO (see `HomologySequenceLemmas.lean`). We supply the `φ.τ₂`
69	version here: it is the engine that proves the horseshoe middle complex `I_B` is an injective
70	*resolution* of `B` (the outer verticals `I_A.ι`, `I_C.ι` are quasi-isos, hence so is the middle
71	augmentation). The proof mirrors Mathlib's `τ₃` argument: a homology four-lemma on the windows of
72	`composableArrows₅`, with the boundary degrees (no predecessor / no successor) handled by
73	`mono_homologyMap_of_mono_of_not_rel` / `epi_homologyMap_of_epi_of_not_rel`. -/
74	
75	namespace HomologicalComplex.HomologySequence
76	
77	open CategoryTheory ComposableArrows Abelian Limits
78	
79	variable {C ι : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] {c : ComplexShape ι}
80	  {S₁ S₂ : ShortComplex (HomologicalComplex C c)} (φ : S₁ ⟶ S₂)
81	  (hS₁ : S₁.ShortExact) (hS₂ : S₂.ShortExact)
82	
83	include hS₁ hS₂ in
84	/-- **Middle-term quasi-isomorphism transfer** (the `τ₂` companion of Mathlib's `quasiIso_τ₃`).
85	If `φ.τ₁` and `φ.τ₃` are quasi-isomorphisms then so is `φ.τ₂`, provided that at each boundary
86	degree (one with no incoming / no outgoing differential) the middle component `φ.τ₂.f i` is a
87	mono / epi respectively. This is the `lean_aux` infrastructure behind
88	`InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact_resolvesMiddle`. -/
89	lemma quasiIso_τ₂ (h₁ : QuasiIso φ.τ₁) (h₃ : QuasiIso φ.τ₃)
90	    (hbMono : ∀ i, (∀ k, ¬ c.Rel k i) → Mono (φ.τ₂.f i))
91	    (hbEpi : ∀ i, (∀ j, ¬ c.Rel i j) → Epi (φ.τ₂.f i)) :
92	    QuasiIso φ.τ₂ := by
93	  have hI1 : ∀ d, IsIso (homologyMap φ.τ₁ d) := fun d => by
94	    rw [← quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]; exact (quasiIso_iff φ.τ₁).1 h₁ d
95	  have hI3 : ∀ d, IsIso (homologyMap φ.τ₃ d) := fun d => by
96	    rw [← quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]; exact (quasiIso_iff φ.τ₃).1 h₃ d
97	  have hE1 : ∀ d, Epi (homologyMap φ.τ₁ d) := fun d => have := hI1 d; inferInstance
98	  have hM1 : ∀ d, Mono (homologyMap φ.τ₁ d) := fun d => have := hI1 d; inferInstance
99	  have hE3 : ∀ d, Epi (homologyMap φ.τ₃ d) := fun d => have := hI3 d; inferInstance
100	  have hM3 : ∀ d, Mono (homologyMap φ.τ₃ d) := fun d => have := hI3 d; inferInstance
101	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
102	  intro i
103	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
104	  have hEpi : Epi (homologyMap φ.τ₂ i) := by
105	    by_cases hi : ∃ j, c.Rel i j
106	    · obtain ⟨j, hij⟩ := hi
107	      apply epi_of_epi_of_epi_of_mono
108	        ((δlastFunctor ⋙ δlastFunctor).map (mapComposableArrows₅ φ hS₁ hS₂ i j hij))
109	      · exact (composableArrows₅_exact hS₁ i j hij).δlast.δlast
110	      · exact (composableArrows₅_exact hS₂ i j hij).δlast.δlast
111	      · exact hE1 i
112	      · exact hE3 i
113	      · exact hM1 j
114	    · have hi' : ∀ j, ¬ c.Rel i j := fun j hj => hi ⟨j, hj⟩
115	      have := hbEpi i hi'
116	      exact epi_homologyMap_of_epi_of_not_rel φ.τ₂ i hi'
117	  have hMono : Mono (homologyMap φ.τ₂ i) := by
118	    by_cases hi : ∃ k, c.Rel k i
119	    · obtain ⟨k, hki⟩ := hi
120	      apply mono_of_epi_of_mono_of_mono
121	        ((δ₀Functor ⋙ δ₀Functor).map (mapComposableArrows₅ φ hS₁ hS₂ k i hki))
122	      · exact (composableArrows₅_exact hS₁ k i hki).δ₀.δ₀
123	      · exact (composableArrows₅_exact hS₂ k i hki).δ₀.δ₀
124	      · exact hE3 k
125	      · exact hM1 i
126	      · exact hM3 i
127	    · have hi' : ∀ k, ¬ c.Rel k i := fun k hk => hi ⟨k, hk⟩
128	      have := hbMono i hi'
129	      exact mono_homologyMap_of_mono_of_not_rel φ.τ₂ i hi'
130	  exact isIso_of_mono_of_epi _
131	
132	end HomologicalComplex.HomologySequence
